---
title: "Lecture 28: Session Tracking using HttpSession"
subtitle: "{{< meta course.number >}} - {{< meta course.title >}}"
author: "{{< meta instructor.name >}}"
date: "March 24, 2026"
code-annotations: below
---

## {#title-slide data-menu-title="Title Slide" background="#053660"}

<div class="custom-title">
[Lecture 28]{.baby-blue-text}
</div>

<div class="custom-subtitle">
[Session Tracking using HttpSession]{.white-text}
</div>

<div class="custom-subtitle3">
Week 9 | Unit III: Servlets<br>
{{< meta course.number >}} - {{< meta course.title >}}<br>
{{< meta instructor.name >}}, {{< meta instructor.title >}}
</div>

---

## {data-menu-title="Learning Objectives"}

[Learning Objectives]{.slide-title}

<hr>

::: {.incremental .body-text-m}
- Explain why `HttpSession` is required in servlet applications
- Create, read, update, and invalidate session attributes
- Distinguish `getSession()` and `getSession(false)` behavior
- Configure timeout and identify session expiration behavior
- Implement login-protected routes using session checks
- Apply safe practices for session handling in exams and labs
:::

---

## {data-menu-title="Why HttpSession"}

[Why HttpSession?]{.slide-title2}

<hr>

::: {.body-text-m}
HTTP is stateless, so server forgets user identity between requests.

`HttpSession` provides per-user server-side state to track:

- login status
- user profile info
- cart or temporary workflow data
:::

---

## {data-menu-title="Create Session"}

[Creating and Using Session]{.slide-title}

<hr>

```{.java code-line-numbers="|1|2|3"}
HttpSession session = request.getSession();
session.setAttribute("loggedInUser", email);
session.setAttribute("role", "student");
```

::: {.body-text-s}
`getSession()` returns existing session or creates a new one if missing.
:::

---

## {data-menu-title="Read Session"}

[Reading Session Safely]{.slide-title2}

<hr>

```{.java code-line-numbers="|1|2|3-5|7"}
HttpSession session = request.getSession(false);

if (session != null) {
  String user = (String) session.getAttribute("loggedInUser");
  String role = (String) session.getAttribute("role");
}

// getSession(false) avoids creating a new empty session
```

---

## {data-menu-title="Session Lifecycle"}

[Session Lifecycle]{.slide-title}

<hr>

```{mermaid}
%%| fig-height: 3.8
flowchart LR
  A[First request] --> B[getSession creates session]
  B --> C[Set attributes]
  C --> D[Subsequent requests use same session id]
  D --> E[Timeout or invalidate]
  E --> F[Session destroyed]
```

::: {.body-text-s}
Session survives across requests until timeout or explicit invalidation.
:::

---

## {data-menu-title="Login Flow"}

[Login and Protected Page Flow]{.slide-title2}

<hr>

```{.java code-line-numbers="|1-5|7|9"}
// In login servlet after credential validation
HttpSession session = request.getSession();
session.setAttribute("loggedInUser", email);
response.sendRedirect("dashboard.jsp");

// In protected servlet/JSP
HttpSession s = request.getSession(false);
if (s == null || s.getAttribute("loggedInUser") == null) {
  response.sendRedirect("login.jsp");
}
```

---

## {data-menu-title="Logout"}

[Logout and Session Invalidate]{.slide-title}

<hr>

```{.java}
HttpSession session = request.getSession(false);
if (session != null) {
  session.invalidate();
}
response.sendRedirect("login.jsp");
```

::: {.body-text-m}
`invalidate()` clears all session attributes and ends that user session.
:::

---

## {data-menu-title="Timeout"}

[Session Timeout Management]{.slide-title2}

<hr>

```{.java}
HttpSession session = request.getSession();
session.setMaxInactiveInterval(20 * 60); // 20 minutes
```

::: {.body-text-m}
- Timeout is based on inactivity
- After timeout, session attributes are no longer available
- User should be redirected to login on expired sessions
:::

---

## {data-menu-title="URL Rewriting"}

[Fallback: URL Rewriting]{.slide-title}

<hr>

::: {.body-text-m}
When cookies are disabled, session tracking can use URL rewriting.
:::

```{.java}
String url = response.encodeURL("dashboard.jsp");
response.sendRedirect(url);
```

::: {.body-text-s}
`encodeURL()` appends session id when required.
:::

---

## {data-menu-title="Common Mistakes"}

[Common Mistakes to Avoid]{.slide-title2}

<hr>

::: {.incremental .body-text-m}
- Calling `getSession()` in every check (creates unnecessary sessions)
- Forgetting null checks when using `getSession(false)`
- Storing sensitive info like passwords in session attributes
- Not invalidating session during logout
- Ignoring timeout behavior in protected routes
:::

---

## {data-menu-title="Exam Focus"}

[AKTU Exam-Oriented Checklist]{.slide-title}

<hr>

::: {.body-text-m}
Prepare these key points:

1. Define session and `HttpSession`
2. Explain `getSession()` vs `getSession(false)`
3. Write login and logout code using session
4. Explain timeout and invalidation
5. Mention URL rewriting as fallback tracking method
:::

---

## {data-menu-title="Summary"}

[Summary]{.slide-title2}

<hr>

::: {.body-text-m}
You can now:

- build simple login tracking with sessions
- protect pages with session checks
- manage logout and timeout cleanly
- answer common servlet session questions in theory and practical exams
:::
